synthesis_text: To reduce the risk of getting COVID-19, several measures are recommended. Practicing good personal hygiene, such as regular hand washing and avoiding touching the face, is crucial [doc1_covid19.txt:chunk_00006]. Using face masks or coverings in public settings helps reduce transmission risk [doc1_covid19.txt:chunk_00006]. Maintaining physical or social distancing is another effective strategy to prevent virus spread [doc1_covid19.txt:chunk_00006]. Ensuring proper ventilation of indoor spaces can reduce airborne virus concentration [doc1_covid19.txt:chunk_00006]. Staying up to date with COVID-19 vaccinations is important to reduce infection risk [doc1_covid19.txt:chunk_00006]. Additionally, avoiding close contact with anyone who is sick or has COVID-19 symptoms is advised [doc2_covid19.txt:chunk_00023].

To reduce the risk of getting COVID-19, it is important to stay up to date on vaccinations, maintain good indoor airflow, practice physical distancing, wear masks in appropriate settings, wash hands frequently, and avoid close contact with sick individuals. These measures are supported by both local and external evidence [doc2_covid19.txt:chunk_00001, doc2_covid19.txt:chunk_00023, doc1_covid19.txt:chunk_00006, doc3_covid19.txt:chunk_00016].

To reduce the risk of getting COVID-19, it is important to follow several preventive measures. These include avoiding crowded places and maintaining at least 1 metre distance from others [doc3_covid19.txt:chunk_00016], wearing a mask in public places where there is community transmission [doc3_covid19.txt:chunk_00016], washing hands frequently with soap and water or using an alcohol-based hand rub [doc3_covid19.txt:chunk_00016], ensuring all indoor spaces are well ventilated [doc3_covid19.txt:chunk_00016], and getting vaccinated while continuing measures to reduce virus spread [doc3_covid19.txt:chunk_00012]. Additionally, staying up to date with COVID-19 vaccinations helps prevent serious illness and death [doc2_covid19.txt:chunk_00001].
